member.name: Wendy Morton
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Wendy Morton is the Conservative MP for Aldridge-Brownhills , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015. She currently undertakes the role of Shadow Minister (Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.answerText: In line with the Code of Practice for Statistics, the Department for Transport has robust procedures in place to lead the Production of Official Statistics under the direction and authority of the Head of Profession for Statistics. As required by the Code...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The department regularly reviews its estate to ensure it meets the needs of customers and colleagues, supports service delivery and represents value for money for the taxpayer. The Workplace Transformation Programme began in 2021 to ensure the department ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what route-by-route performance data the Department holds on short-formed passenger rail services across all operators; what assessment has been made of the impact of short formation on passenger overcrowding,
writtenQuestions.answerText: Department for Transport Operator (DFTO) typically monitors levels of short formations at an operator level every four weeks and therefore is not able to provide data for each route. DFTO expects operators to provide appropriate capacity in response to ex...

writtenQuestions.heading: Taxation: Debt Collection
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what estimate HM Revenue and Customs has made of (a) the number of individuals expected to be subject to the proposed lower-value debt recovery powers in each of the next five financial years, (b) the average amount
writtenQuestions.answerText: The vast majority of taxpayers pay in full and on time. Most debts are resolved as taxpayers engage with HMRC and agree a way forward. The Government's proposals are intended to ensure fairness for taxpayers who pay what they owe by tackling a small minor...

writtenQuestions.heading: Gibraltar: UK Relations with EU

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Rt Hon Member to the answers I provided to her and other Members on this subject at departmental oral questions on 21 April and in the debate held in Westminster Hall on 8 July 2026 where I answered questions from the Rt Hon Member.

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Government Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether her Department maintains an internal list or other assessment of local authorities considered to be at heightened risk of issuing a section 114 notice or requiring Except
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department undertakes regular engagement with local councils, including on their financial positions. The final 2026-27 Local Government Finance Settlement makes available £78 billion in Core Spending Power for local authorities in England in 2026-27,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what steps he is taking to help mitigate the potential impact of extreme temperatures.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cabinet Office works closely with experts in the Met Office and UKHSA to continually assess the forecast and review heat health alerts. Ahead of periods of forecast extreme temperatures, the Cabinet Office convenes the cross-government Severe Weather ...

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Rt Hon Member to the answers I provided to her and other Members on this subject at departmental oral questions on 21 April.

writtenQuestions.heading: Gibraltar: UK Relations with EU
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Leader of the House, whether he has had discussions with the Foreign Secretary on arrangements for a debate on the Gibraltar Treaty during the period of parliamentary scrutiny under the Constitutional Reform and Governance Act 2010.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government’s commitment to holding debates during the process under the Constitutional Reform and Governance Act 2010 is subject to the request being made in a timely manner and parliamentary time being available.There are of course other routes for H...

writtenQuestions.heading: Gibraltar: UK Relations with EU
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment she has made of the Foreign Affairs Committee's views on Parliamentary scrutiny timetable for the Gibraltar Treaty.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Rt Hon Member to the answers I provided to her and other Members on this subject at departmental oral questions on 21 April.

writtenQuestions.heading: Gibraltar: UK Relations with EU
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, if she plans to seek the views of parliamentarians on the Gibraltar Treaty before agreeing to the application of the Treaty.
